name: Tex-Mex Turkey Chili
recipeInstructions:
1. Place turkey thighs on a chopping board and slice the meat away from the bones.
2. Cut the meat into 1-inch cubes and discard the skin.
3. Spray a non-stick pot (or pressure cooker) with cooking spray for no-fat frying.
4. Add the cubed meat to the pot and brown over moderate heat in its own melted fat (no added oil needed).
5. Remove the pot from the heat and stir boiling water into the juices in the pot.
6. Pour the liquid into a cup, set aside until fat rises to the surface.
7. With a bulb-type baster, skim off and discard the surface fat.
8. Return the fat-skimmed liquid to the pot (bones may be added for flavor and removed before serving).
9. Add all remaining ingredients except cheese.
10. Cover and simmer over low heat until tender, about 1 hour in a conventional pot or 20 minutes in a pressure cooker.
11. Uncover and continue to simmer until most of the liquid evaporates and the chili is thick.
12. Top with cheese.
